title: Values, Variables, Constants, Types, Zero Values, and Pointers · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A configuration loader must distinguish omitted values, explicit zeroes, constants, aliases, defined types, conversions, and optional fields. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a typed configuration and value-semantics model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use booleans, strings, integers, floating-point, complex values, bytes, runes, and their zero values with explicit domain meaning.
Choose var, short declaration, assignment, multiple assignment, and lexical scope while detecting shadowed state.
Use typed and untyped constants, iota, representability, and explicit conversion without hiding unit or overflow risk.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
